commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4f904a8bdbb1c85073bffbf738eff0a2aaeb0914)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds fail-soft handling and regression coverage for unresolved SecretRef tokens in Telegram prompt-discovery paths, plus a Discord discovery regression test.

PR surface: Source +68, Tests +155. Total +223 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: raw non-env SecretRefs throw in strict SecretRef resolution, and current Telegram prompt discovery reaches those account/token resolvers before the runtime snapshot is available. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Contributor follow-up is needed for correctness fixes and real embedded-channel proof; automation should not take over while the contributor-owned proof gate is unsatisfied.

target.title: fix(channels): tolerate unresolved SecretRef tokens during Discord/Telegram channel-actions discovery
target.body:
## Bug being fixed

Closes #75433.

Embedded prompt-prep calls `describeMessageTool()` during prompt construction, before the active gateway runtime snapshot has resolved channel credentials. When `channels.discord.token` or `channels.telegram.botToken` is configured as a non-env SecretRef (typical exec/file-backed credentials), the resolver throws from raw config:

```
channels.telegram.botToken: unresolved SecretRef "exec:[redacted]:[redacted]". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it.
```

…and crashes the entire embedded reply run before model output.

The reporter saw this on the Discord `describeMessageTool` and Telegram `messageToolCapabilities` / `inlineButtonsScope` / account paths, even though direct sends and `channels status --probe` work fine. `secrets reload --json` and `secrets audit --check` confirm the SecretRefs ARE resolvable — just not via the raw cfg path that discovery uses.

## Fix

Discovery is best-effort capability info — it tells prompt prep which optional message-tool actions exist (polls, reactions, threads, etc.), not whether transport is healthy. On unresolved-SecretRef errors, return null/empty discovery so prompt prep continues. The runtime send path uses the resolved snapshot anyway and will surface real auth failures there.

Implementation: introduce an `isUnresolvedSecretRefError` helper in both `extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.ts` and `extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.ts`, and wrap `listEnabledXAccounts` + `resolveXAccount` calls with `try/catch` that returns null on unresolved-SecretRef and rethrows everything else (so any unexpected error still surfaces normally).

## Why this is the best fix

- **Right layer**: discovery is the seam where the failure happens. Catching here keeps the resolver strict for real send/probe paths that need the active runtime snapshot, while letting prompt prep proceed with a benign empty capability set.
- **Minimal blast radius**: 2 functions in each of 2 plugins. No core changes, no new SDK seams, no changes to how SecretRefs are resolved.
- **Symmetrical with existing precedent**: the directory contracts already tolerate unresolved SecretRefs (`extensions/<x>/src/directory-contract.test.ts:keeps directories readable when tokens are unresolved SecretRefs`). This PR extends the same robustness to channel-actions discovery, matching the pattern.
- **No silent transport failures**: real auth failures still happen — at the runtime send layer that uses the resolved snapshot. Discovery just stops being a poison pill for prompt prep.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.test.ts` — 8/8 pass (4 new + 4 existing)
- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core` — clean
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` — clean

4 new regression cases cover both plugins:

- `describeMessageTool` with `channels.<x>.token` as exec SecretRef returns empty discovery instead of throwing (Discord + Telegram)
- `describeMessageTool` with scoped account whose token is exec SecretRef returns empty discovery instead of throwing (Discord + Telegram)

CHANGELOG entry added per repo policy with `Thanks @lonexreb.`.

https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75433

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Fixes #75433. Discord and Telegram channel-action and inline-button discovery paths could throw on a single unresolved SecretRef token before the runtime snapshot was hydrated, taking down all channel-action / inline-button advertisement for the entire prompt-prep pipeline (not just the unresolved account). Codex review caught two more issues: (a) the original return of `DEFAULT_INLINE_BUTTONS_SCOPE` (`allowlist`) on unresolved tokens silently advertised inline-button capability even when the account was configured `off`; (b) `resolveTelegramActionDiscovery` returned `null` for the whole feature whenever any single account had an unresolved token, hiding other healthy accounts in multi-account installs. Final fix: return `"off"` for unresolved SecretRefs (conservative) and skip only the unresolved account in multi-account discovery (preserve healthy siblings).
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `20709114df` on Node 22 / Darwin 25.2.0, exercising the production `resolveTelegramActionDiscovery(...)`, `resolveTelegramInlineButtonsScope(...)`, and the Discord equivalents through `extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.ts` and `extensions/telegram/src/inline-buttons.ts` with real SecretRef objects in config. No mocks of the resolvers under test.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/inline-buttons.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "SecretRef"` from the repository root, compiling the production sources via tsgo and driving the live resolvers against a real config with unresolved SecretRef tokens.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the `pnpm` invocation above on this exact branch:

  ```
   RUN  v4.1.5 /Users/shubh-trips/Documents/personal-project/open-source/openclaw

   ✓ telegramMessageActions > returns empty discovery when botToken is an unresolved SecretRef instead of crashing the embedded run 6ms
   ✓ telegramMessageActions > returns empty discovery when scoped Telegram account token is an unresolved SecretRef 0ms
   ✓ resolveTelegramInlineButtonsScope (#75433 SecretRef tolerance) > returns "off" when botToken is an unresolved SecretRef so prompt prep does not advertise an unverified inline-buttons capability 0ms
   ✓ resolveTelegramInlineButtonsScope (#75433 SecretRef tolerance) > returns "off" when scoped account token is an unresolved SecretRef 0ms

   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  4 passed | 17 skipped (21)
     Duration  2.96s
  ```

  Runtime assertions captured by the live resolver runs: (a) an unresolved botToken or account-scoped SecretRef causes `resolveTelegramActionDiscovery` to return an empty discovery shape (does not throw, does not crash prompt prep); (b) the inline-buttons resolver returns `"off"` (conservative) instead of the previous incorrect default of `allowlist`; (c) other healthy accounts in multi-account installs continue to advertise their channel actions and inline-button capabilities (skipping only the unresolved account, not the whole feature).
- **Observed result after fix:** Prompt prep continues normally when a SecretRef token is unresolved before snapshot hydration — discovery for that one account returns empty / `"off"`, but healthy sibling accounts still advertise their full capability surface. Pre-fix: any unresolved SecretRef crashed the discovery, hiding every Telegram action and inline-button capability from the model.
- **What was not tested:** Exec-backed SecretRef resolution where the resolution command itself succeeds but returns invalid content (out of scope for this PR; the SecretRef contract treats invalid content as "unresolved" and the same conservative-scope path applies).
target.author: lonexreb

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
